topics.name: 100 - 200 Level: Introduction to Mass Communication and News Writing
topics.description: MAC 101 Introduction to Mass Comm, History of Nigerian Media, and News Reporting.

subtopics.name: MAC 101: Introduction to Mass Communication
objectives: Understand mass communication theories, models (Lasswell, Shannon-Weaver), and media functions
Trace history of print and broadcast media in Nigeria from 1859 Iwe Irohin to present

subtopics.name: MAC 201: News Writing and Reporting
objectives: Apply lead writing, inverted pyramid style, interviewing techniques, and news values

topics.name: 300 Level: Public Relations, Advertising, and Broadcasting
topics.description: MAC 301 Public Relations, Advertising, Radio/TV Production, and Photojournalism.

subtopics.name: MAC 301 & MAC 302: Public Relations and Advertising
objectives: Design public relations campaigns, press releases, crisis management, and media relations
Execute advertising strategy, copy writing, layout design, and media planning

subtopics.name: MAC 303: Radio and Television Production
objectives: Produce radio programs, audio editing, television news scripting, camera operations, and video editing

topics.name: 400 Level: Media Law, Ethics, Digital Media, and B.Sc. Project
topics.description: MAC 401 Media Law & Ethics, Digital Journalism, Development Comm, and Research Project.

subtopics.name: MAC 401: Media Law, Ethics, and Digital Media
objectives: Understand libel, defamation, sedition, copyright law, Freedom of Information (FOI) Act, and NBC codes
Apply digital journalism techniques, online publishing, data journalism, and social media management

subtopics.name: MAC 499: Final Year Mass Communication Research Project
objectives: Execute empirical content analysis, survey, or audience research and defend thesis
